query: i want to take a look at bktide... i ran snapshot on a build that didn't exist on a pipeline, and got an esoteric error rather than an indicator it was missing. use https://buildkite.com/myorg/my-pipeline/builds/1234 as the example
look at the last 20 builds on https://buildkite.com/myorg/my-app on main and tell me which specs are flaking most
try running `bk auth status` sandboxed -- i want to debug why it's failing in the sandbox and figure out what env vars the auth flow needs
my pipeline in buildkite is stuck at the deploy step for 20 minutes. pull the build logs and check the agent state

i queued a CI job via our internal tool that created https://buildkite.com/myorg/my-pipeline/builds/1234/steps/canvas. it failed CI as expected, but I'm not seeing the follow-up build starting from it. there's a service that listens for buildkite events to queue up new jobs
we launched a new tool yesterday. look at its buildkite builds and summarize success/failure rates for users over the last week
the rails app build b123 failed on main again. figure out if it's the same flaky spec from last week or something new. pull the build page on buildkite
one team's pipeline yaml has a deprecation i want to remove. check what pipelines touch that file and whether removing it keeps CI green on the PR
bktide is giving weird errors when i query a pipeline by name instead of slug. figure out if that's a bktide bug or a buildkite api thing
help me reproduce a failing buildkite test locally. the job runs `bundle exec rspec` but with some flags i can't figure out from the build logs
i want to add a new pipeline step to .buildkite/pipeline.yml for running rubocop on changed files only. where do i put the plugin config?
my github actions workflow is failing because the node version isn't being set correctly. help me fix the workflow yaml
in my Rust CLI tool i want to refactor the inspect command to use a trait instead of an enum. look at src/commands/inspect.rs and sketch the change
review https://github.com/myorg/my-cli/pull/564 and leave comments about the error handling patterns
review the buildkite plugin's investigating-builds SKILL.md. it's not triggering reliably and i want to tighten the description to catch bk CLI invocations
resolve merge conflicts on https://github.com/myorg/my-cli/pull/453 against main. don't run any tests, just the merge
set up my local ~/.bk/config.yml with my org and default pipeline to my-pipeline
pull down pickled-claude-plugins main and check open PRs, especially anything touching the buildkite plugin's skills
in my rails app, add a new enum to the deprecation model for tracking which team owns it
in pickled-claude-plugins, add a new reference doc under plugins/buildkite/skills/investigating-builds/references/ for common bktide errors
